chunk_id: GSK_papers_2021/Filled forms, Ashwini and Anastasiya, DRAFTS /University of Helsinki - Data Integrity RFI_AN_NC.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: GSK_papers_2021/Filled forms, Ashwini and Anastasiya, DRAFTS /University of Helsinki - Data Integrity RFI_AN_NC.docx
text:
Good Data Management 
Data Integrity Expectations
Data integrity ensures processes are in place to assure data is Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original and Accurate, Complete, Consistent, Enduring and Available (ALCOA-CCEA). Systems and processes should be in place to prevent situations that might impact data integrity and in turn the robustness of decisions based upon those data. 
Questions align to the principles of ALCOA CCEA
Your Company Legal Entity Name | University of Helsinki
Your Name | Dr. Anniina Färkkilä
Date completed | 11.1.2021
No. | Data Integrity Expectations | Yes/No | Description of how the data integrity expectations will be met
1 | Will all experiment write-ups have a unique ID and a clearly documented purpose? | Yes
2 | Will all experimental methodologies including equipment and material details be fully documented? | Yes
3 | Will information about the test material be sufficient to ensure its identification? E.g. Compound number | Yes
4 | Will data be recorded accurately and legibly?
If data are not located in a lab notebook, is there a suitable alternative? 
If yes, is there a reference to its location? | Yes
Yes 
Yes | All the data are stored digitally in a hard drive and secured University servers.
5 | Where data are observational and require subjective assessment, will systems be in place to avoid bias?
For studies involving animals will measures such as randomisation and blinding be used to minimise/avoid bias? | Yes
n/a | Whenever possible all the data will be validated using non-biased computational methods or analysis by multiple researchers.
6 | Will statistical methods and/or any software used be fully recorded? E.g. Method names, software & version numbers | Yes

chunk_id: GSK_papers_2021/Filled forms, Ashwini and Anastasiya, DRAFTS /University of Helsinki - Data Integrity RFI_AN_NC.docx::chunk_0001
source_file: GSK_papers_2021/Filled forms, Ashwini and Anastasiya, DRAFTS /University of Helsinki - Data Integrity RFI_AN_NC.docx
text:
Whenever possible all the data will be validated using non-biased computational methods or analysis by multiple researchers.
6 | Will statistical methods and/or any software used be fully recorded? E.g. Method names, software & version numbers | Yes
7 | Will any selective use of data for inclusion and/or exclusion be fully justified and recorded? E.g. removal of statistically justified outliers | Yes | Inclusion / exclusion of the data outliers will be discussed in the lab notebook and the research article / report.
8 | Will data (paper or electronic) be collected into an appropriate long term secure location? Please describe how this is this managed, for example, 
Please describe document retention policies
Please describe processes to ensure paper and electronic data is retained and secured
Please describe any processes for data transfer, if applicable | Yes | All the paper data and documents are stored in a locked cabinet at the laboratory. In addition, they are also stored in electronic form in a University server with restricted access. Data transfer can be enabled via access to secured University server.
9 | Will data trails exist so that conclusions can be traced back to raw data (enabling reconstruction of experiments)? | Yes | Links to all the raw data are stored electronically at the University server.
10 | Will any of the work for this engagement/contract be sub-contracted to other 3rd parties?
If yes, how will you ensure the integrity of the data generated by the sub-contractor? | Yes/No | Some of the work will be carried out by using the internal University of Helsinki core facility services.

e for the chemical substance in question. Chemical substances are handled according to their MSDS. Toxic substances are handled in the fume hood by trained personnel wearing the protective gear. Describe the engineering controls (e.g. fume cupboards) available for handling hazardous materials. | The research facilities are equipped with fume hoods, and the flow intensity is regularly inspected by technical staff.
Describe any other controls (administrative or PPE) to minimize exposure to hazardous materials. | Laboratory coats, protective gloves and safety goggles are always used when performing laboratory experiments.

Please describe the engineering controls (e.g. biosafety cabinets) available for handling bio hazardous agents | The research facilities are equipped with fume and laminar hoods, and the flow intensity is regularly inspected by technical staff. Laminar hood surfaces are disinfected with UV irradiation twice daily, and regularly wiped with 70 % ethanol.
Describe any other controls (administrative or PPE) to minimize exposure to bio hazardous agents. | Bio-hazardous materials are handled with extra care, for example, by using long-sleeved thicker gloves, laboratory coats, and safety goggles. Bio-hazardous waste, surgical instruments and dishes in contact with the bio-hazardous material are treated using 2% Virkon solution in water according to manufacturer’s instructions followed by autoclave sterilization (instruments and lab ware). The disinfected solid wastes are burnt.
Describe the occupational health arrangements in place to monitor staff that handle biohazardous agents. | University of Helsinki provides regular health checkups for its research personnel.

4. First-aid measures
If exposed to excessive levels of dusts or fumes, remove to fresh air and get medical attention if
cough or other symptoms develop. If breathing is difficult, trained personnel should give oxygen.
Inhalation
Immediately flush skin with plenty of water. Take off contaminated clothing and wash before reuse.
Get medical attention if symptoms occur.
Skin contact
Rinse thoroughly with plenty of water for at least 15 minutes and consult a physician.Eye contact

6. Accidental release measures
Keep unnecessary personnel away. Keep people away from and upwind of spill/leak. Use only
non-sparking tools. Dust deposits should not be allowed to accumulate on surfaces, as these may
form an explosive mixture if they are released into the atmosphere in sufficient concentration.
Wear appropriate protective equipment and clothing during clean-up. Use a NIOSH/MSHA
approved respirator if there is a risk of exposure to dust/fume at levels exceeding the exposure
limits. Ensure adequate ventilation. Local authorities should be advised if significant spillages
cannot be contained. For personal protection, see section 8 of the SDS.

## Page 3
Eliminate all ignition sources (no smoking, flares, sparks, or flames in immediate area). Take
precautionary measures against static discharge. Use only non-sparking tools. Avoid dispersal of
dust in the air (i.e., clearing dust surfaces with compressed air). Collect dust using a vacuum
cleaner equipped with HEPA filter. Stop the flow of material, if this is without risk.
Large Spills: Wet down with water and dike for later disposal. Shovel the material into waste
container. Following product recovery, flush area with water.
Small Spills: Sweep up or vacuum up spillage and collect in suitable container for disposal.
Never return spills to original containers for re-use. For waste disposal, see section 13 of the SDS.

containment and cleaning up

7. Handling and storage
Keep away from heat/sparks/open flames/hot surfaces. - No smoking. Ground/bond container and
receiving equipment. Minimize dust generation and accumulation. Avoid significant deposits of
material, especially on horizontal surfaces, which may become airborne and form combustible dust
clouds and may contribute to secondary explosions. Routine housekeeping should be instituted to
ensure that dusts do not accumulate on surfaces. Dry powders can build static electricity charges
when subjected to the friction of transfer and mixing operations. Provide adequate precautions,
such as electrical grounding and bonding, or inert atmospheres. Explosion-proof general and local
exhaust ventilation. Avoid prolonged exposure.

y charges
when subjected to the friction of transfer and mixing operations. Provide adequate precautions,
such as electrical grounding and bonding, or inert atmospheres. Explosion-proof general and local
exhaust ventilation. Avoid prolonged exposure. Wear appropriate personal protective equipment.
Observe good industrial hygiene practices.
Precautions for safe handling
Keep away from heat, sparks and open flame. Keep containers tightly closed in a dry, cool and
well-ventilated place.

Vakuutus 
Allekirjoituksellani vakuutan, että tavarat eivät tule yksinomaan myynninedistämistarkoituksessa tehtävään tutkimukseen, 
analyysiin tai kokeeseen. 
Tiedän seuraavat asiat: 
- Tavaroita saa käyttää vain tässä ilmoitettuun tarkoitukseen. 
- Tavarat, joita ei ole kulutettu loppuun tai hävitetty tutkimusten, analyysien tai kokeiden aikana, tai joita ei ole 
tosiasiallisesti käytetty, on Tullin suostumuksella ja valvonnassa hävitettävä tai tehtävä kaupallisesti arvottomiksi, 
luovutettava valtiolle ilman kuluja tai vietävä yhteisön ulkopuolelle. 
- Jos tutkimusten, analyysien tai kokeiden jälkeen jäljelle jäänyt tavara otetaan muuhun käyttöön, siitä on maksettava 
tulli. Tulli kannetaan muuhun käyttöön ottamisen päivänä voimassa olevan tullikannan mukaan ja Tullin kyseisenä

text:
Good Data Management 
Data Integrity Expectations
Data integrity ensures processes are in place to assure data is Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original and Accurate, Complete, Consistent, Enduring and Available (ALCOA-CCEA). Systems and processes should be in place to prevent situations that might impact data integrity and in turn the robustness of decisions based upon those data. 
Questions align to the principles of ALCOA CCEA

4 | Will data be recorded accurately and legibly?
If data are not located in a lab notebook, is there a suitable alternative?
